description: Provider-backed campaign comparing Kubernetes default scheduling, resource-aware custom scheduling, and resource plus network-aware custom scheduling for gateway-routed distributed LocalAI worker-mode workloads.

cycleGovernance.governancePolicy: Compare scheduler modes under paired logical scenarios where infrastructure, model mix, LocalAI worker count, traffic access mode, namespace labels and telemetry prerequisites are held constant.

scope.primaryQuestion: Does resource plus network-aware scheduling produce more suitable LocalAI master-worker placement than Kubernetes default scheduling and resource-only custom scheduling when tenant traffic and inter-node latency make network cost observable?

latencyMatrixValidationPolicy.reason: The active network-latency.<node> annotations must match the configured C9 inter-group matrix before scheduler decisions are refreshed.

infrastructureSummarySemantics.workerNodeCount: All C9 variants use the 4-worker-node provider-backed infrastructure profile.
infrastructureSummarySemantics.schedulerMode: Varied across DEFAULT, LOADAWARE and NETAWARE variants within each logical scenario.
